proved: The public Draft 2020-12 schema is strict and aligned with valid remote and local typed fixtures.
The semantic validator rejects undisclosed remote transmission and non-HTTPS remote destinations.
Storage-disabled and zero-retention states are separate, and positive or negative attestation must carry a matching typed boolean.
The validator rejects raw prompts, outputs, credentials, and raw provider payloads anywhere in a record.
Terminal state, chronological timestamps, unique source snapshots, token totals, and finite cost are cross-checked.
The compiled Syzygy MCP embeds the same strict provider-run schema and reports its validator implemented.
The production frontend bundle and the full Rust unit suite pass with the embedded contract.
notProved: Product model execution emits an authoritative record.
A provider honored a storage, training, or zero-retention policy.
Remote provider credentials or disclosure UI are product-wired.
Provider usage and cost are accurate against a live invoice.
Adversarial multi-provider execution consumes and reconciles these records.
